verses.text: The law has dominion over a man while he lives, as a wife is bound while her husband lives. A binding tie. Death alone ends the law's claim.
The law has dominion over a man while he lives, as a wife is bound while her husband lives. A binding tie. Death alone ends the law's claim.
The law has dominion over a man while he lives, as a wife is bound while her husband lives. A binding tie. Death alone ends the law's claim.
Ye are become 'dead to the law by the body of Christ,' to bring forth fruit unto God. Freed to serve. We die to the law to live for God.
Ye are become 'dead to the law by the body of Christ,' to bring forth fruit unto God. Freed to serve. We die to the law to live for God.
Ye are become 'dead to the law by the body of Christ,' to bring forth fruit unto God. Freed to serve. We die to the law to live for God.
Is the law sin? God forbid; 'I had not known sin, but by the law'; sin revived, and I died. Law reveals sin. It shows the evil it cannot cure.
Is the law sin? God forbid; 'I had not known sin, but by the law'; sin revived, and I died. Law reveals sin. It shows the evil it cannot cure.
Is the law sin? God forbid; 'I had not known sin, but by the law'; sin revived, and I died. Law reveals sin. It shows the evil it cannot cure.
The commandment 'ordained to life' I found unto death; yet 'the law is holy.' Good law, dead self. The fault is in us, not the law.
The commandment 'ordained to life' I found unto death; yet 'the law is holy.' Good law, dead self. The fault is in us, not the law.
The commandment 'ordained to life' I found unto death; yet 'the law is holy.' Good law, dead self. The fault is in us, not the law.
The law is spiritual, 'but I am carnal, sold under sin.' Sold to sin. The fallen heart cannot keep the holy law.
The law is spiritual, 'but I am carnal, sold under sin.' Sold to sin. The fallen heart cannot keep the holy law.
'What I would, that do I not; but what I hate, that do I.' Inner war. The will is overborne by indwelling sin.
'What I would, that do I not; but what I hate, that do I.' Inner war. The will is overborne by indwelling sin.
'What I would, that do I not; but what I hate, that do I.' Inner war. The will is overborne by indwelling sin.
In my flesh 'dwelleth no good thing'; to will is present, but how to perform I find not. Helpless. Good resolved, but not achieved.
In my flesh 'dwelleth no good thing'; to will is present, but how to perform I find not. Helpless. Good resolved, but not achieved.
In my flesh 'dwelleth no good thing'; to will is present, but how to perform I find not. Helpless. Good resolved, but not achieved.
I delight in the law of God 'after the inward man,' but a law in my members wars against it. Two laws. The believer feels the conflict of two natures.
I delight in the law of God 'after the inward man,' but a law in my members wars against it. Two laws. The believer feels the conflict of two natures.
I delight in the law of God 'after the inward man,' but a law in my members wars against it. Two laws. The believer feels the conflict of two natures.
'O wretched man that I am! who shall deliver me?... I thank God through Jesus Christ.' The cry and answer. Only Christ delivers from this body of death.
'O wretched man that I am! who shall deliver me?... I thank God through Jesus Christ.' The cry and answer. Only Christ delivers from this body of death.
